artifact_identity.exact_byte_hash_scope: Generated in-memory text outputs at build time. projection-status.json cannot self-embed its own final byte digest; all other generated outputs are exact.

html_route_sharding.purpose: Keep first-read reference routes compact while preserving no-JS static detail pages.
html_route_sharding.runtime_policy: first_routes_do_not_require_lazy_full_text_packets; bulk corpus remains explicit and lazy

legacy_compatibility.machine_readable_policy: Old JSON packets, download files, generated assets, and projection-status routes remain machine-readable files. Only old human HTML pages use visible transition HTML; validation parses JSON routes and fails if they return HTML.

mode_rows.label: Machine-readable aliases
mode_rows.policy: Machine-readable JSON and static assets are emitted as files so the old URL keeps its content type.

mode_rows.label: Legacy projection status
mode_rows.policy: The old projection-status URL returns a legacy JSON status pointer, not an HTML redirect.

mode_rows.label: Legacy security.txt
mode_rows.policy: The old host serves its own security.txt so vulnerability-reporting metadata remains valid while the human-facing site is a tombstone.

mode_rows.label: Human HTML transitions
mode_rows.policy: HTML routes show the transition and may forward to the canonical route; they are not used for machine-readable assets.

mode_rows.label: Legacy 404 fallback
mode_rows.policy: The GitHub Pages 404 is a visible compatibility fallback, not a server-side redirect.

canonical_kernel.note: The single public query kernel. Every derived view below is a projection of this packet and carries this source_fingerprint; none re-projects source independently.

derived_views.adds: per-record search text plus area, rules-and-ideas, paper-module, and page records
concise Markdown guide from quickstart, overview, what-it-is, architecture, and doctrine page bodies, plus evidence and source routes
lightweight public site JSON map: reader contract, route index, source-map refs, sidecar refs, and projection summary
page manifest, rule-lattice counts, coverage-gap counts, and graph-scene summary
canonical public object identity, routes, typed omissions, and per-kind coverage assertions
CSP-safe script transport for the object coverage panel
agent-readable doorway over public object families and source-safe maps
default AI handoff: question routes, component/family digests, claim ceilings, and provenance without the heavy visible-text bodies
lazy exact-text JS wrapper used only when a local file:// preview must force a download instead of opening JSON in a tab
advanced full-text AI review capsule: read_me_first, answer contract, preflight, starter prompts, question router, view descriptors, and the full deep record strata in one self-describing file
lazy exact-text JS wrapper used only when a local file:// preview must force a download instead of opening JSON in a tab
old/new route classes, JSON alias policy, visible HTML transition policy, and legacy 404 contract

graph_scene_kernel.note: The graph-topology projection; carries its own scene source fingerprint.

count_semantics.emitted_route_count: Generated HTML page outputs, including compatibility aliases.
count_semantics.route_alias_count: Compatibility HTML outputs that resolve to a canonical page object.

public_export_egress_posture.authorization_boundary: The standalone public Plectis source slice and review packets are public distribution artifacts in populated-source release mode. That authorization does not publish the private macro root, private raw media, account material, future cohorts, follow-ups, live services, or any material absent from the public repository/review packet boundary.

class_rules.prohibited_secret_or_live_payload: Secret material and live non-public data are excluded from the public packet; any finding in this class blocks distribution until removed.
class_rules.public_by_design: Public repository URLs, public page routes, stable public component ids, evidence modes, scope limits, and public contact routes intended for review.
class_rules.public_optional_personal_data: Name, project email, public professional links, and student-budget context only when they serve contact or reviewer-calibration purposes.
class_rules.strategic_private_origin_metadata_requir: Non-secret internal coordinates such as private-origin root slugs, state paths, implementation module paths, internal tool paths, source-body hashes, line counts, and capability symbols. Default action is project to a public handle or omit; keep only with an explicit allowlist receipt.

current_public_contact_decision.reason: Retained fields let reviewers identify and contact the maintainer; exact age is unnecessary personal data for the public packet.
retained_fields: University of Bristol economics undergraduate context

optional_context_fields: University of Bristol economics undergraduate context
student-budget context when explaining support constraints
non-affiliation statements needed to avoid institutional endorsement

public_runtime_rule.runs_real_tools_meaning: runs_real_tools describes whether the public component command invokes external machinery.

public_export_egress_posture.release_authority_boundary: This authorizes distribution of the standalone public source slice and review packets only; it does not grant hosted-service, production-security, provider-affiliation, professional-advice, trading/investment, formal-proof correctness, source-mutation, release, or private-root-equivalence authority.

strategic_metadata_policy.current_public_packet_posture: Strategic-coordinate scanner hits are review-blocking for hostile distribution until a human allowlists them or the builder projects them to public handles.

readback_contract.safe_refresh_blocker: source_coupling.status must be clear before landing generated site outputs

count_semantics.projection_row_count: all rendered roster rows, including alternate paper-module detail rows

quality_examples.label: component-standard-runtime-result record coverage as projection-only evidence
local pressure rows as typed candidates for owner-surface refinement

records.reason: The old Field guide carried the same roster role as Reference; keep only a route alias.
The Reference index is the scan-then-route roster over reference objects.
Components owns component cards, filters, evidence summaries, and map routes.
Doctrine reference owns the rule lattice and synopsis-to-body routing.
Paper modules owns long-form paper-module reader bodies and diagram routing.
Glossary owns public vocabulary cards and term relationships.
Open Questions, Source, Updates, Contact, and Vulnerability disclosure describe the site/repository surface rather than the reference object roster.

stale_when: source hashes change without a rebuild; --check --validate fails until the site is regenerated and current.
